source.title: How to send a direct message in Slack

interaction: Select a DM recipient (Jagvir Brar) in the sidebar, type a short reply into the message composer ('Hey @Jagvir Brar can we reschedule our meeting?'), press the send arrow -- the message posts into the thread with the sender name (Steven Pelham) and a 2:31 PM timestamp under a 'Today' divider.
note: Acquired via the yt-dlp+ffmpeg fallback (ladder step 3) after two higher-priority ladder steps were unavailable this session: (1) native-local Slack.app is installed and already signed in per reference/sources.json, but the computer-use request_access call for Slack was denied by the user this session, so no interaction could be driven/recorded on the desktop app; (2) the web client (app.slack.com/client) has no active browser session (workspace-signin screen appeared) and logging in is a hard no per task rules. This official Slack YouTube tutorial shows the same story class (select conversation -> type -> send -> message commits with avatar/name/timestamp) so it calibrates composer-to-post timing/easing even though it's a DM rather than a channel. Clip/frames are gitignored (local-only); this manifest is the tracked record.
